summary: Matched the ftCommon HammerWait slice by replacing most of asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s with a new src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_HammerWait.c and header. The matched file reports 1.92 KB at 100.00% across HammerWait callbacks and helper functions. The PR also cleaned up prototypes/headers, typed hammer-related fighter/common-data fields, added missing item/player prototypes, added the new object to obj_files.mk, and adjusted report_score.py title output for 100% matches.
what_changed: Removed 576 lines of HammerWait assembly from asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s and introduced a 222-line C implementation in src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_HammerWait.c.

Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_HammerWait.h with typed prototypes for ftCo_800C4E4C, ftCo_800C4E94, ftCo_800C4ED8, ftCo_800C4F64, ftCo_HammerWait_Anim, ftCo_HammerWait_IASA, ftCo_HammerWait_Phys, ftCo_HammerWait_Coll, ftCo_800C511C, ftCo_800C5240, ftCo_800C5284, ftCo_800C52F4, ftCo_800C53E4, ftCo_800C544C, ftCo_800C548C, ftCo_800C54C4, ftCo_800C5500, ftCo_800C554C, ftCo_800C555C, and ftCo_800C55CC.

Moved the matched HammerWait declarations out of src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.h and improved nearby remaining prototypes such as ftCo_800C576C, ftCo_800C5A50, and ftCo_800C5D34 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures.
Changed ftCommonData x6AC and x6B8 from UNK_T to int and changed Fighter::x2330 from Vec2 to IntVec2, matching integer countdown/timer usage in HammerWait.

Implemented HammerWait behavior around hammer item ownership/state: HammerWait motion changes, IASA checks, physics/collision callbacks, hammer-kind detection via It_Kind_Hammer, timer initialization from p_ftCommonData->x6AC and it_80293DCC, hammer state updates through it_80293E34/it_8026C16C/it_8027B070, and bonus handling via pl_800403C0.
Added missing declarations for it_8027B070 in src/melee/it/it_2725.h and pl_800403C0 in src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.h.
Updated many ftCommon and fighter source files to include ftCo_HammerWait.h where they reference the moved HammerWait symbols.
Added semicolons after many RETURN_IF(...) macro invocations across touched fighter files, apparently to support or normalize the macro call style used by the new C code.
Added the new ftCo_HammerWait.c object to obj_files.mk.
Adjusted tools/report_score.py so generated PR titles omit the percentage when a file is fully matched, producing titles like Match `ftCo_HammerWait` (1.92 KB).
smart_moves: Used small static inline helpers inlineA0 and inlineB0 to express duplicated assembly patterns: HammerWait motion-state setup and a shared animation/collision item callback path used by both ftCo_800C4F64 and ftCo_HammerWait_Anim.
Typed x2330 as IntVec2 after observing the assembly treated offsets 0x2330 and 0x2334 as integer counters rather than floats; this made decrement and equality checks natural in C.
Kept uncertain symbols address-based where no better names were evidenced, while giving callback names already known from action tables, such as ftCo_HammerWait_Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll.
Separated the newly matched HammerWait API into ftCo_HammerWait.h instead of leaving those declarations in the broad ft_0C31.h bucket, reducing dependency ambiguity for later work.

Preserved motion-state flag behavior by factoring ftCo_800C54C4 to return a combined Ft_MF_SkipHit/Ft_MF_SkipModel/Ft_MF_SkipMatAnim/Ft_MF_SkipRumble/Ft_MF_SkipColAnim/Ft_MF_UpdateCmd/Ft_MF_Unk19 mask only when already in a HammerWait-to-HammerLanding motion range.
Used RETURN_IF chains in ftCo_HammerWait_IASA to mirror short-circuit input/action checks compactly.
decomp_lessons: If an offset is used with integer loads/stores, decrement operations, and zero comparisons, revisit the struct type instead of forcing float/vector semantics; here x2330 became IntVec2 and common data x6AC/x6B8 became int.

When decompiling a slice from a catch-all asm/header file, move matched declarations into a domain-specific header and update includes at call sites. This avoids leaving stale UNK_RET prototypes in the legacy bucket.
Duplicated assembly bodies can often be matched more cleanly with static inline helpers shared by two exported functions; HammerWait used this for the motion setup and item/collision callback logic.
Matching a state file may require small header improvements in adjacent systems, such as item and player bonus libraries, because calls formerly hidden in assembly need real prototypes.
For action-state callbacks, keep the standard Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll naming where known, but do not over-name address-only helpers without evidence.
A matching PR can legitimately include mechanical style edits in dependent files when a macro-call convention changes or becomes necessary for compilation; keep these changes broad but simple.
naming_conventions: Common fighter state files use the ftCo_ prefix and state names like ftCo_HammerWait.c / ftCo_HammerWait.h.
Action callbacks follow the suffix pattern _Anim, _IASA, _Phys, and _Coll, e.g. ftCo_HammerWait_Anim.
Unknown helper functions retain address-based names such as ftCo_800C511C, ftCo_800C52F4, and ftCo_800C54C4.
Header guards in this area use GALE01-address-style names, e.g. GALE01_0C4E4C for ftCo_HammerWait.h.
Function declarations include address comments like /* 0C4E4C */ before the prototype.
Motion-state enum names use ftCo_MS_*, such as ftCo_MS_HammerWait and ftCo_MS_HammerLanding.
Item kind constants use It_Kind_* naming, shown by It_Kind_Hammer.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced the assembly block for ftCo_800C4E4C through ftCo_800C555C with C and left later ft_0C31 functions still in the old header/assembly area.
Used static inline helper inlineA0 to force matching codegen for the shared Fighter_ChangeMotionState sequence and subsequent ftCo_800C4E94 setup.

Used static inline helper inlineB0 to match the identical logic in ftCo_800C4F64 and ftCo_HammerWait_Anim: branch on fp->x2338.x, otherwise compare cur_anim_frame against zero and frame_spd_mul before calling ft_800892A0, ft_80089824, and it_8027B070.
Expressed assembly offset 0x2330/0x2334/0x2338/0x233C accesses as fp->x2330.x, fp->x2330.y, fp->x2338.x, and fp->x2338.y after correcting the struct types.

Used exact motion/flag constants in C rather than raw immediates where available, while still preserving raw value 106 for the ftCo_800C0694/ftCo_800BFFD0/ftCo_800C0200 interaction because no named constant is evidenced in the slice.
The match report lists every function in ftCo_HammerWait.c at 100.00%, giving strong evidence that the C code matched the removed assembly.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that name ftCo_800C5408, ftCo_800C5588, or the raw value 106 used by HammerWait.
Check whether x2330/x2338 fields later received semantic names for hammer timers/state flags.
Search for subsequent PRs matching the remaining HammerWalk, HammerTurn, HammerJump, HammerFall, and HammerLanding functions in ft_0C31.
Look for PRs that further refined RETURN_IF macro style or removed mechanical semicolon churn.
Investigate item hammer functions it_80293DCC, it_80293E34, and it_8027B070 for later naming or prototype improvements.
